

# Coil
$\alpha_{u}=\alpha_{i}+90^{o}$ -> The current lags the voltage by 90°

$Z=sL \boxed{Z_{L}(j\omega)=j\omega L}$ $Z_{L}=j^{10}$?

$I=\frac{U}{Z_{L}}$

# capacitor
$\alpha_{i}=\alpha_{u}+90^{o}$ -> The current leads the voltage by 90°
$Z=\frac{1}{sC}$
$Z_{L}=\frac{1}{j\omega c}=-j \frac{1}{\omega c}$

$Z=R+jX$
Z-impedance
R-resistance
X-reactance

$\sqrt{ R^{2}+x^{2} }·e^{ j\phi }$

$Y=G-jB$
Y-admittance
G-conductance
B-susceptance

Example 1

$Z=R_{1}+j\omega L_{2}+\frac{-j \frac{1}{\omega C_{4}}·R_{3}}{R_{3}-j \frac{1}{\omega C_{4}}}$=
=$5+j 3+ \frac{2.5(-j 5)}{2.5-j 5}$ $/·\frac{2.5+j 5}{2.5+j 5}$
=$5+j_{3}+ \frac{62.5-j 31.25}{31.25}$=
=$5+j 3+2-j 1$=
=$7+j 2$ - Equivalent impidance

$j 2=j\omega L$
$L=2mH$
ciul
capacitor - current leads voltage
coil - current lags voltage

---
A two-terminal circuit consists of resistor and a single energy storage element (L or C) in series. Find the circuit's reactance, X (provide the value in Ω and the correct sign), given the instantaneous voltage and current:

$i(t)=6\sin(9t+45°)$
$u(t)=18\sqrt{ 2 }(9t)$

$U=\frac{U}{I}=\frac{18}{\frac{6}{\sqrt{ 2 }}e^{ 45° }}=3\sqrt{ 2 }e^{ -j 45° }=3\sqrt{ 2 }\left( \frac{\sqrt{ 2 }}{2}-\frac{j\sqrt{ 2 }}{2} \right)=$
=$\overbrace{ 3 }^{ R }-j \overbrace{ 3 }^{ X }$

$U=18e^{ j0 }$
$I=\frac{6}{\sqrt{ 2 }}e^{ j 45° }$

Answer: -3

---

It is know that the resistor rms voltage is 7V and the capacitor rms voltage is 5V.
Find the 5mH coil rms voltage, in V.

We can asume that one variable has 0 phase angle

9"></path></g></g><mask></mask></svg>

$|U|=\sqrt{ 7·4 }=8.6V$

---

A series RC circuit draws an RMS current of 1 A that leads the applied voltage by 45°. The amplitude of voltage is 400 V at an angular frequency of $\omega$=50 000 rad/s. What is the capacitance, in µF?

$I=1e^{ j 0 }$
$U=\frac{400}{\sqrt{ 2 }}e^{ -j 45° }$

$Z=\frac{U}{I}=\frac{400}{\sqrt{ 2 }}e^{ -j 45 }=\frac{400}{\sqrt{ 2 }}\left( \frac{\sqrt{ 2 }}{2}-j \frac{\sqrt{ 2 }}{2} \right)=200-j 200$
$Z=R-jX_{c}$
Reactance
$X_{c}=\frac{1}{\omega c}$
$Z_{c}=-j \frac{1}{\omega c}$

---

A voltage source is given by $e(t)=26 \sqrt{ 2 }\sin(6t)$.
A series RL circuit with R=1Ω draws an RMS current of 2A from this source. Find the inductance L

E=26
$|I|=2A$
$Z=R+jX_{L}$
$R=1Ω$

$|Z|=\frac{|E|}{|I|}=\sqrt{ R^{2}+X^{2}_{L} }=$
$13=\sqrt{ 1+X^{2}_{L} }$
$X_{L}=13Ω$

$\omega L=13$
$L=2.167H$

---

The rms currents of RLC parallel circuit are: $I_{R}=2A$ $I_{C}=7A$ $I_{L}=7A$
Find the supply (total ) rms current in A
2A

---

A coil has and inductive reactance of j500 Ω. What magnitude of capacitive reactance, $|X_{C}|$, in Ω, must be connected in series with it so that when the series combination is supplied with U=1000V, the voltage across the coil rises to 1.3U (1300V)?
